engine.note: Findings are computed from org-wide read-only sweeps; savings claims follow the engine's honesty rules (requests-only pricing, bound-units-only claims, rate basis on every figure).

generator.name: confighub-operational-app-export
generator.regeneration_policy: Regenerate with the same or a newer generator version, then run node lifecycle.mjs upgrade --from <regenerated-dir> to diff the regeneration against local modifications.

next_gate: Set CONFIGHUB_ORG, register the browser OAuth client, run oauth:smoke, sign in, verify /api/me, bind the ConfigHub action authority, run the domain finder, create and inspect preview --finding <id>, record the short-lived review with the authenticated Cub identity, then explicitly confirm commit for that review id; controller and runtime proof remain separate.
non_claims: The export did not mutate ConfigHub, a controller, or Kubernetes.
The export wrote the app under the declared destination and updated workflow assistant's user-scoped fleet inventory; any Git commit or push is a delivery step, not live operational proof.

The generic browser apply endpoint remains blocked; the CLI can execute one finding-owned action after an authenticated short-lived review and separate explicit confirmation, but provider-native atomic expected-revision enforcement is not yet available and remains visible in the receipt.
Fleet registration recorded the app in the org-level index shape; the fleet-record Unit itself is created through the governed path, not by this export.

oauth_client.policy: One browser client per app per org; the client id is public, the registration is fleet inventory. Registration, redirect-URI changes, rotation, and revocation are recorded in the fleet record; cross-org access is out of scope for this client.

ui_tool.flow: OIDC Authorization Code with PKCE, followed by ConfigHub token exchange discovered from /api/info.
ui_tool.name: ConfigHub Custom UI Apps JavaScript SDK

sessionLifecycle.appRule: App code never stores tokens and never clears browser storage on load; the auth provider owns all session state.
sessionLifecycle.pinNote: 0.1.1 is the newest @confighub/react-auth on npm; raise the pin to ^0.2.0 once it is published. The provider owns the whole lifecycle, so the raise needs no app changes.
sessionLifecycle.refresh: The auth provider re-authenticates silently on a fresh page load (an OIDC prompt=none redirect against the identity provider session); a failed silent attempt degrades to the sign-in screen, never an error.

ui_tool.tokenPosture: The SDK keeps the minted ConfigHub token in memory; browser storage only ever holds non-credential state (transient PKCE state and a returning-login marker).
